feed.title: onlinetest release feed
feed.subtitle: New releases and changelog feed of onlinetest
feed.rights: All rights reserved 2022, Peter Hedenskog and team

entry.title: onlinetest 4.0.1
entry.summary:
Fixed
- Compare page now renders screenshots, the filmstrip and the run video when the HAR points at a different sitespeed.io result server (e.g. wikiperformance comparing HARs that live on wikimedia.sitespeed.io). The helmet CSP only allowed 'self' for images and — via the inherited default-src — for video, so the cross-origin URLs the HAR carries in _meta.screenshot / _meta.filmstrip[].file / _meta.video were blocked and the slots came up empty. img-src now also allows any https: origin and a new media-src does the same for video #269.

entry.title: onlinetest 4.0.0
entry.summary:
Added
- New failed_reason TEXT and finished_date TIMESTAMP columns on sitespeed_io_test_runs. failed_reason is populated from three paths the DB previously knew nothing about: Bull's global:failed event (the testrunner threw), the queue-down submit path in add-test.js (Redis unreachable when the test was queued), and the result-queue path when sitespeed.io itself exits non-zero (testrunner now sends Test runner exited with code N: <last line of stderr>, capped at 500 chars). finished_date is stamped on every terminal transition, so end-to-end duration can be derived as finished_date - added_date and run duration as finished_date - run_date (which is the browsertime start timestamp, not finish) #264.
- server/database/migrations/ directory establishes the convention for schema changes against existing deployments — setup.sql only runs on a fresh Postgres data dir, so live databases need ALTERs applied separately. Each migration ships with a .sh helper (check / backup / dry-run / apply / verify / rollback) that runs psql inside the compose Postgres container, plus a README documenting the manual path. Existing deployments must run 001-add-failure-fields.sh apply before rolling the server forward #264.

entry.title: onlinetest 3.8.0
entry.summary:
Added
- Default sitespeed.io Docker image bumped from 40 to 41 in .env.example. The README "Update sitespeed.io version" snippets now reference 41 / 42 as the current and next-major tags.
- Vendored compare bundle rolled to pagexray 5.0.0 (HTTP/3 detection, case-insensitive Cache-Control directives, missingCompression over-count fix). The bundle shrinks from ~28 KB to ~17 KB thanks to pagexray's switch from uglify-es to terser #262.

Fixed
- /admin "failed" pill is now a real health signal: it counts failed test runs in the last 24 h from Postgres instead of Bull's getJobCounts(), which was capped by removeOnFail and only decayed on a successful retry — so the pill accumulated weeks of stale failures and shouted "13 failed" next to activity cards that correctly showed 0 in 24 h #261.
- Compare cookie list no longer renders domains like .example.com\nUIDR=... when a HAR concatenates multiple Set-Cookie response headers into one newline-joined value — the next cookie's name was leaking into the previous cookie's Domain= attribute. Picked up via the pagexray bump #262.

entry.title: onlinetest 3.7.0
entry.summary:
Added
- Per-queue 24 h sparkline on each /admin queue row, independently scaled so low- and high-traffic queues both show shape #256.
- Subtle motion on /admin: one-time staggered bar-chart entrance, refresh-indicator pulse, and tweened number rollups when totals change. All honour prefers-reduced-motion #256.

Fixed
- Queue defaults bumped to removeOnComplete: 50 / removeOnFail: 100 / attempts: 2; code-level fallbacks aligned with the yaml #255.
- Internal queues (testrunners, result) no longer listed on /admin — they had no failures, no sparkline, and an inert Empty button #258.
- Refresh indicator on /admin now sits in a fixed-width column so the version pill can't shift sideways each tick #258.
- Testrunner setup duplicated on restart — registration was concatenating old + new instead of deduping #257.
- Multi-type setups on one location (e.g. desktop + emulatedMobile) collapsed into the last entry after a restart; dedupe now keys on full setup identity, not just queue name #259.

entry.title: onlinetest 3.6.0
entry.summary:
Added
- Activity stats on /admin: totals for the last 24 hours and 7 days plus a stacked bar chart of tests per hour over the last 24 h (completed / failed / other). Pure server-rendered SVG, cached for 60 s so the auto-refresh doesn't beat on Postgres #252.

Fixed
- Hide the Failed count for the internal testrunners and result queues — the number was unactionable because the queues don't appear in Recent failures #251.
- Age labels in Recent failures now graduate to d/mo past 48 h instead of shouting 12608h ago #251.
- Long URLs no longer push the /search/ results table past its container and shove the page off-centre. URL cells now wrap at any character; the form has overflow-x: auto as a safety net #253.

entry.title: onlinetest 3.5.0
entry.summary:
Added
- Health banner on /admin: Redis up/down, Postgres up/down, connected testrunners, totals for pending/running/failed jobs #247.
- "Currently running" table on /admin — every active job with URL/script, queue, runner, and elapsed time. Runs over 10 minutes are highlighted #247.
- "Recent failures" table on /admin with the error message and a one-click Retry button #248.
- Active and Failed counts on the queue table, plus a red "no worker" badge when a queue has pending jobs but no testrunner serving it #246.
- /admin auto-refreshes every 15 seconds. Pauses in background tabs #249.
- Opt-in auto-refresh toggle on /search/, 60 seconds. Off by default. Pauses while any test is ticked, so Compare selections aren't lost. State remembered in localStorage #250.
- /admin mobile layout reworked for the new tables — rows stack into name → counts → action on narrow screens #246 #247 #248.

entry.title: onlinetest 3.4.0
entry.summary:
Added
- Connected testrunners table on /admin showing hostname, location, the setup each machine serves, and a fresh / stale "last seen" badge. Each testrunner publishes a heartbeat on the existing testrunners Bull queue every 30 s; the server tracks lastSeenAt per runner and prunes anything quiet for >120 s, so crashed runners (OOM, host reboot, docker kill) drop off the admin page within ~2 minutes instead of haunting the metrics gauge forever. When two runners pick the same LOCATION_NAME + deviceId, the server logs a clear Testrunner queue collision: hostA and hostB both claim queue X. Change LOCATION_NAME (or deviceId) on one of them. — the registration still goes through so existing deployments don't break, but the collision is no longer silent #244.
- deploy/PRODUCTION.md gets an "Adding a third (or Nth) testrunner" section with three concrete troubleshooting cases: not registering, stale row, queue collision #244.
- Compare workflow on /search/: the column over the per-row checkbox is now its own Compare header (split out from the generic "Actions"), a one-line helper below the table explains the affordance, and each checkbox carries an aria-label / title of "Select this run to compare with another". The Compare button itself starts disabled, gates submission until exactly two completed runs are ticked (a third tick is refused — the unchecked boxes go disabled when the limit is hit), and shows a transient inline message on a premature click ("Select 1 more test to enable Compare." / "Select 2 completed tests above first.") via aria-disabled + a polite aria-live region #240.
- deploy/update.sh — a single operator command that pulls the latest images, restarts services with up -d --remove-orphans, prints docker compose ps, and tails logs for 10 seconds so first-boot errors land in the same terminal. --mode {all-in-one|server|testrunner} picks the right production compose file; --version X.Y.Z rewrites SITESPEED_IO_SERVER_VERSION / SITESPEED_IO_TESTRUNNER_VERSION in .env atomically before pulling. PRODUCTION.md is updated to call the helper in every place it used to spell out the compose invocation by hand.
- .env.example is now the single source of truth for every variable any compose file reads (dev or production). New header walks through the three deployment shapes, and the previously-undocumented DOMAIN variable (used by the Caddy single-server compose for automatic HTTPS) is included. PRODUCTION.md drops its inline env-var snippets and points at .env.example so the two can't drift.

Fixed
- SIGTERM / SIGINT now actually drain in-flight HTTP requests before the process exits. The previous shutdown chain ended at this.app.close() on the Express app object (no such method exists), so the HTTP server was never told to stop — it just disappeared when process.exit(0) fired a few lines later, killing in-flight requests mid-response. The web server now keeps the real http.Server reference and calls close() on it; a configurable server.shutdown.timeoutMs (default 30 s) caps the drain and falls back to closeAllConnections() after the deadline. A second signal during shutdown short-circuits to immediate exit for operators who'd rather not wait #243.
- Custom hero / navbar logo overrides now use whatever dimensions the operator sets in html.logo.*. Two hardcoded aspect-ratio: 90.488 / 19.543 / aspect-ratio: 123 / 25.375 CSS rules were forcing every logo back to the bundled SVG's proportions #239.
- The analyze button on the Extras tab no longer sits a few pixels lower than on the other tabs. #extras .control was applying padding: 4px 0 to the submit button's wrapper as well as to the intended checkbox rows; scoped to .extras-fieldset .control so only the diagnostics checkboxes get the extra spacing #239.
- Footer spacing: removed the duplicate   before "version" and added one between the trailing dash and "AGPLv3", so the footer now reads "©sitespeed.io 2026 - version X.Y.Z - AGPLv3" with consistent spacing #239.
- Search help panel: tighter copy. "You can combine your search commands. To find a test for a URL, date and browser you can search like this" is now "Combine commands to narrow your search. Example". The "Find tests by …" preamble that repeated on every line is gone — each command now leads with its field name and (where applicable) the allowed values #239.

Security
- Stop logging and echoing back invalid API keys. validateKey used to write the rejected value to the server log and return it in the 403 response body, so typos and stale secrets piled up in log aggregation. The log line is now Invalid API key supplied (no value, no length) and the response carries a single "Invalid API key." message in every locale #242.
- Close two XSS vectors that the recent UI work uncovered:
- Enforce a defense-in-depth CSP (default-src 'self'; script-src / style-src allow 'unsafe-inline' transitionally because pug templates still ship inline blocks; frame-ancestors 'none', object-src 'none', base-uri 'self', form-action 'self') and re-enable HSTS. Both were disabled previously #242.
- Block sensitive sitespeed.io flags from the GUI's "Command line args" tab. The user-supplied extras textarea used to forward arbitrary flags to the testrunner with only --verbose filtered. A GUI submission could load plugin / JS code (--plugins.add, --browsertime.preScript), run any binary as the browser (--browsertime.chrome.binaryPath), redirect HARs and metrics to an attacker-controlled destination (whole --graphite.* / --s3.* / --gcs.* / --scp.* / --slack.* / --matrix.* / --datadog.* / --influxdb.* namespaces), or read arbitrary files via --config. A denylist now drops these tokens (and their value arguments) before they reach sitespeed.io, and /sitespeed-help.json filters the same set so the CLI picker stops suggesting them (277 → 187 entries). API submissions (which pass a userConfig object, not extras) are deliberately untouched #241.

entry.title: onlinetest 3.3.1
entry.summary:
Fixed
- Filmstrip 404s in the compare view, again — the 3.3.0 pass still constructed frame URLs on a fixed 100 ms cadence inside (FirstVisualChange, LastVisualChange), which broke whenever visual progress sat flat between two boundaries (the expected JPGs were never written to disk). The vendored compare bundle now reads frame timestamps straight from _visualMetrics.VisualProgress — one JPG per VP sample, named after that exact ms — and lets the existing 100 ms padding step forward-fill the uniform grid, so the rendered strip still reads "nothing happened for 2 s" differently from "everything changed in 50 ms" #238.

entry.title: onlinetest 3.3.0
entry.summary:
Added
- Refreshed the vendored compare bundle with a sharing pass, accurate transfer-size diffs and style recalculation visibility:

Fixed
- Filmstrip and Visual Progress thumbnails no longer 404 — frame timestamps are now derived from the same _visualMetrics.VisualProgress change points sitespeed.io's own report uses, matching the cadence sitespeed.io actually wrote to disk.
- Millisecond timings in the page-x-ray diff are rounded to whole ms — the new recalc duration field reports sub-ms precision ("17.099 ms") that's noise at this scale.
- "Switch HAR" and run-dropdown changes preserve the previous source URLs / config / stripVersion choice, so the Share UI doesn't downgrade from "Copy share link" to "Download bundle" on every toggle.

entry.title: onlinetest 3.2.0
entry.summary:
Added
- Refreshed the vendored compare bundle to pick up the upstream regression-spotting pass:
